responsibilities: Perform Building Inspector / Certifier duties in accordance with organisational policies, industry standards, and applicable legislation.
Maintain safe work practices and comply with health, safety, and environmental requirements relevant to Construction & Infrastructure.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver quality outcomes.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, and reporting as required.
Support continuous improvement and professional development within the role.
duties: Carry out core Building Inspector / Certifier tasks as directed by work orders, specifications, and supervisor instructions.
Apply technical knowledge and practical skills required for the Building Inspector / Certifier role.
Inspect own work for quality and compliance with standards before sign-off.
Use tools, equipment, and systems safely and report defects or hazards promptly.
Communicate progress, issues, and completion status to the supervisor or team lead.
Follow site or workplace policies including PPE, access control, and emergency procedures.
Participate in toolbox talks, briefings, and training as required.
Maintain a clean, organised, and safe work area.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee asbestos removal worker operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Access, Safety & Specialist workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Identify, survey, and assess asbestos-containing materials (ACM) in buildings and structures per the Health and Safety at Work (Asbestos) Regulations 2016.
Carry out Class A and Class B licensed asbestos removal works per the approved asbestos removal control plan.
Establish and maintain asbestos removal enclosures, airlocks, decontamination units, and negative pressure units for friable asbestos removal.
Remove and bag asbestos-containing materials using approved wet methods, tools, and disposal procedures.
Carry out clearance inspection and air monitoring after asbestos removal to verify re-occupation safety.
Dispose of asbestos waste materials at approved disposal facilities per waste transfer documentation requirements.
Wear, fit-test, don, and doff appropriate respiratory protective equipment (RPE) including P2 and P3 respirators.
Decontaminate tools, equipment, and PPE following asbestos removal works using approved procedures.
Maintain all asbestos removal equipment including negative pressure units and decontamination showers in operational condition.
Complete all required asbestos removal documentation including removal control plans, air monitoring records, and waste transfer documents.
Hold a current asbestos removal worker certificate issued by a WorkSafe NZ approved training provider.
Comply with all HSWA 2015 requirements, Health and Safety at Work (Asbestos) Regulations 2016, and WorkSafe NZ Approved Codes of Practice.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee demolition worker operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Access, Safety & Specialist workstream.

Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Carry out manual and mechanical demolition of buildings, structures, and infrastructure per demolition plans and WorkSafe NZ requirements.
Operate demolition equipment including excavators with demolition attachments, skid steer loaders, and high-reach demolition machines.
Carry out selective demolition and deconstruction works maintaining structural stability of retained elements.
Identify, manage, and safely remove hazardous materials including asbestos-containing materials (ACM), lead paint, and PCBs per the Health and Safety at Work (Asbestos) Regulations 2016.
Sort, segregate, and manage demolition waste and recyclable materials per waste management plans.
Erect hoarding, fencing, and exclusion zones around demolition sites to protect the public and adjacent properties.
Carry out ground remediation, site clearance, and preparation works including rubble removal and backfilling.
Read and interpret demolition drawings, hazardous materials registers, and site-specific safety plans accurately.
Ensure all demolition works comply with the Building Act 2004, HSWA 2015, and relevant WorkSafe NZ guidelines.
Operate and maintain all demolition plant, hand tools, and PPE in a safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site noise, vibration, dust, and environmental controls required by resource consents and council requirements.
Complete all required site documentation, hazardous materials records, and waste transfer documentation.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee rope access technician operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Access, Safety & Specialist workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Carry out inspection, maintenance, cleaning, repair, and construction works at height using industrial rope access techniques per IRATA or SPRAT certification.
Rig and de-rig rope access systems including anchor systems, descenders, ascenders, and traverse lines per the rope access work plan.
Conduct visual and non-destructive inspection of building facades, bridges, towers, wind turbines, and infrastructure at height.
Carry out concrete repair, crack injection, caulking, painting, and surface treatment works using rope access techniques.
Install, maintain, and test fall arrest systems, anchor points, and permanent access systems on structures.
Carry out window cleaning, facade cleaning, and building maintenance works using rope access and abseil techniques.
Rescue incapacitated workers from rope access positions using approved rope rescue techniques.
Inspect and maintain all rope access equipment including ropes, harnesses, descenders, and connectors per manufacturer requirements.
Prepare rope access method statements, risk assessments, and rescue plans for all rope access works.
Ensure all rope access works comply with WorkSafe NZ working at heights guidelines and IRATA/SPRAT Code of Practice.
Carry out rope access works under the direct supervision of a Level 3 Rope Access Supervisor as required by certification.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use all specified PPE and rescue equipment.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee scaffolder operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Access, Safety & Specialist workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Erect, alter, and dismantle tube and coupler, modular system, and frame scaffold structures per AS/NZS 1576 and scaffold design plans.
Install edge protection systems including handrails, mid-rails, toe boards, and safety netting per HSWA 2015 and WorkSafe NZ requirements.
Erect cantilevered scaffolds, suspended scaffolds, rolling tower scaffolds, and birdcage scaffolds to approved design drawings.
Install protective fans, catch fans, and debris netting on scaffold structures adjacent to public areas and roads.
Carry out scaffold inspections at required frequencies and complete scaffold inspection tags and registers.
Erect and dismantle temporary propping and shoring systems for structural support during construction and demolition.
Read and interpret scaffold design drawings, erection sequences, and structural load calculations accurately.
Ensure all scaffold works comply with AS/NZS 1576, HSWA 2015, and WorkSafe NZ guidelines for scaffolding.
Operate materials hoists, gin wheels, and lifting equipment to handle scaffold components at height safely.
Carry out all scaffold works under a current Scaffolding Certificate of Competence issued by a recognised NZ authority.
Maintain all scaffold equipment, components, and tools in safe and serviceable condition and tag defective components.
Report any scaffold defects, unauthorised modifications, or unsafe conditions to the Site Supervisor promptly.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee traffic management coordinator operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Access, Safety & Specialist workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Prepare, submit, and implement Traffic Management Plans (TMP) for construction works per NZTA Code of Practice for Temporary Traffic Management (CoPTTM).
Set out and maintain temporary traffic control devices including signs, cones, barriers, and variable message signs per approved TMP.
Direct and control traffic flows through and around construction work zones to ensure safety of road workers and the public.
Carry out traffic management site audits and inspections to verify compliance with the approved TMP.
Brief and supervise traffic controllers (TC) and ensure they hold current Site Traffic Management Supervisor (STMS) or TC qualifications.
Liaise with road controlling authorities, police, emergency services, and network utilities regarding traffic management requirements.
Respond to incidents, emergencies, and unplanned events within traffic management zones safely and effectively.
Monitor traffic conditions, queue lengths, and public complaints and adjust traffic management arrangements as required.
Maintain all traffic management equipment in a reflective, clean, and serviceable condition.
Complete all required CoPTTM records, TMP registers, audit records, and incident reports.
Hold a current Site Traffic Management Supervisor (STMS) qualification issued by a recognised NZ training provider.
Comply with all Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 obligations, CoPTTM requirements, and road controlling authority conditions.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee bridge & infrastructure builder operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Civil & Earthworks workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Carry out civil construction works for bridges, culverts, retaining walls, seawalls, wharves, and other infrastructure structures.
Erect, align, and strip formwork for bridge decks, piers, abutments, and wing walls per structural engineer drawings.
Install precast bridge beams, deck units, and superstructure elements using cranes and specialist lifting equipment.
Install bridge bearings, expansion joints, drainage systems, and parapets to design specifications.
Carry out underwater works including cofferdam construction, pile installation, and substructure concrete in marine environments.
Install geosynthetic reinforcement, drainage layers, and select fill for reinforced earth retaining wall systems.
Carry out concrete placement, compaction, and finishing for structural bridge elements to specified tolerances.
Monitor structural movements, settlements, and displacements using survey instruments and monitoring equipment.
Carry out maintenance and rehabilitation works on existing bridges including bearing replacement and concrete repair.
Read and interpret structural drawings, erection sequences, and construction methodology plans accurately.
Ensure all works comply with NZTA Bridge Manual, relevant NZ Standards, and structural engineer specifications.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and maritime safety requirements.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee drainlayer operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Civil & Earthworks workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Install, maintain, and repair below-ground drainage systems including stormwater, wastewater, and subsoil drainage pipework.
Carry out excavation, trench preparation, pipe laying, bedding, backfilling, and compaction to specified grades and depths.
Install drainage pipes, fittings, and appurtenances including uPVC, HDPE, concrete, and ductile iron pipework.
Install manholes, inspection chambers, sumps, soakage systems, and stormwater detention tanks per specifications.
Connect new drainage systems to existing council reticulation networks in accordance with local authority requirements.
Carry out CCTV drainage inspections, drain tracing, and blockage clearing using jetting equipment and drain rods.
Perform pressure testing, air testing, and water testing of installed drainage systems prior to backfilling.
Ensure all drainlaying works are carried out under a current Licensed Drainlayer registration issued by the PGDB New Zealand.
Read and interpret civil engineering drawings, drainage layout plans, and council standard drawings accurately.
Liaise with local councils, building inspectors, and project engineers for drainage inspections and CCC.
Operate excavators, compactors, and laser levels safely per site traffic management and Health and Safety plans.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space entry procedures.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee earthworks / plant operator operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Civil & Earthworks workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Operate heavy earthmoving plant including excavators, bulldozers, graders, scrapers, and compactors for bulk earthworks and site formation.
Carry out excavation for foundations, trenches, basements, retaining walls, roads, and underground services to specified dimensions.
Operate wheeled loaders, skid steer loaders, and telehandlers for material handling, stockpiling, and loading operations.
Carry out compaction of fill materials using smooth drum rollers, padfoot rollers, and plate compactors to specified density.
Operate tip trucks and water carts for material transport, watering, and dust suppression operations.
Carry out subgrade preparation, road base laying, and pavement construction for roads and hardstand areas.
Read and interpret civil engineering drawings, earthworks plans, and survey setout pegs to execute earthworks accurately.
Operate plant safely near underground services including identification, hand exposure, and appropriate safe working distances.
Carry out plant pre-start checks, daily maintenance, and defect reporting per manufacturer requirements.
Comply with Traffic Management Plans (TMP), site traffic rules, and Health and Safety at Work Act 2015.
Hold current operator certificates for all plant operated, including relevant WorkSafe NZ high-risk work licences.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and environmental controls at all times.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee paving & asphalt layer operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Civil & Earthworks workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Lay, compact, and finish asphalt and bituminous surfacing for roads, carparks, driveways, footpaths, and airfields to specified thickness and tolerances.
Operate paving machines, asphalt rakers, and compaction rollers to achieve smooth and uniform pavement surfaces.
Prepare subgrade and basecourse materials including trimming, compaction, and proof rolling prior to asphalt paving.
Apply prime coat, tack coat, and bituminous emulsions to prepared surfaces prior to asphalt placement.
Cut, trim, and repair existing asphalt surfaces including patching, overlay, and mill-and-fill operations.
Install kerb and channel, drainage channels, and road furniture in conjunction with pavement construction.
Carry out line marking, thermoplastic road markings, and pavement delineation as required.
Read and interpret civil engineering drawings, pavement design drawings, and road construction specifications accurately.
Ensure all pavement works comply with NZTA specifications, local authority requirements, and project quality plans.
Manage traffic control and site establishment in accordance with approved Traffic Management Plans (TMP).
Operate asphalt plant, paving equipment, and roller compactors safely per HSWA 2015 requirements.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including heat-resistant footwear.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee piledriver / foundation specialist operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Drive, bore, and install piles including precast concrete, steel H-piles, CHS piles, bored cast-in-situ piles, and screw piles for foundations.
Operate pile driving rigs, drop hammers, hydraulic hammers, vibratory hammers, and diesel hammers to drive piles to specified set or refusal.
Carry out CFA, rotary bored, and driven cast-in-place pile installation using specialist drilling rigs.
Install ground anchors, soil nails, micropiles, and jet grout columns for retaining walls and slope stabilisation.
Carry out dynamic pile load testing and static pile load testing per engineer specifications.
Install sheet piles, king post walls, secant pile walls, and contiguous pile walls for excavation support.
Read and interpret geotechnical reports, pile design drawings, and installation specifications accurately.
Monitor pile installation records including set, driving logs, concrete volumes, and grout injection pressures.
Ensure all piling works comply with AS 2159 (Piling) and geotechnical engineer specifications.
Operate cranes, piling rigs, and supporting plant safely per NZ crane operator licensing requirements.
Carry out site preparation, temporary works, and dewatering associated with piling works.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and noise and vibration management plans.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee tunnelling / underground worker operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Civil & Earthworks workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Carry out underground excavation works including drill and blast, roadheader, and TBM-assisted tunnelling for road, rail, and utility tunnels.
Operate underground drilling rigs, jumbo drills, and explosives charging equipment for rock excavation per approved drill and blast designs.
Install ground support systems including rock bolts, mesh, shotcrete, and steel ribs to engineer-designed ground support patterns.
Apply shotcrete (sprayed concrete) to tunnel walls and crowns using wet or dry process spray equipment.
Carry out excavation mucking using underground loaders, haul trucks, and conveyor systems.
Install precast concrete segmental lining, grouting, and waterproofing systems for bored tunnels.
Operate shaft sinking equipment, stage winches, and kibble systems for vertical shaft construction.
Monitor tunnel deformation, convergence, and ground movement using survey instruments and automated monitoring systems.
Handle, store, and use explosives and detonators per the HSNO Act and site-specific blasting procedures.
Carry out gas monitoring, ventilation management, and emergency procedures in underground environments.
Ensure all underground works comply with WorkSafe NZ underground mining regulations and project safety management plans.
Comply with all underground Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including self-rescuers.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee data / communications cabling technician operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Electrical & Low Voltage workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Install, terminate, test, and commission structured cabling systems including Cat 5e, Cat 6, Cat 6A, and fibre optic cabling.
Install telecommunications racks, cabinets, patch panels, outlets, and containment systems per TIA-568 and AS/NZS 3080.
Terminate and test copper data cabling using keystone jacks, patch panels, and cable testers.
Splice, terminate, and test fibre optic cables using fusion splicers, mechanical connectors, and OTDR test equipment.
Install and commission IP CCTV systems, access control systems, intercom systems, and audio-visual systems.
Install antenna systems, satellite dishes, and associated cabling for broadcast and communications applications.
Install and commission nurse call systems, PA/emergency warning systems, and building automation cabling.
Carry out cable management, labelling, and documentation of all installed cabling systems per project specifications.
Read and interpret building services drawings, cable schedules, and telecommunications specifications accurately.
Ensure all cabling installations comply with AS/NZS 3080 (Telecommunications Installations) and AS/NZS 3000.
Maintain all test equipment, fusion splicers, and cable installation tools in a calibrated and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee electrician operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Install, test, commission, and maintain electrical wiring systems, switchboards, distribution boards, and associated equipment per AS/NZS 3000 and NZBC.
Install and connect electrical cables, conduits, trunking, cable trays, and containment systems for all installation types.
Install, wire, and commission lighting systems, power outlets, switches, circuit breakers, RCDs, and metering equipment.
Carry out installation and commissioning of three-phase and single-phase motor circuits, control panels, and industrial equipment.
Perform fault finding, diagnosis, and rectification on existing electrical installations using appropriate test instruments.
Install data cabling, communications wiring, fire alarm systems, security systems, and other low-voltage systems.
Carry out all electrical work under a current Electrical Worker Registration (EWR) issued by the EWRB New Zealand.
Ensure all electrical installations are inspected and certified by a Registered Electrical Inspector (REI) with Certificates of Compliance issued.
Read and interpret electrical drawings, single-line diagrams, schematics, and specifications accurately.
Liaise with the lines company, metering provider, and relevant authorities for connections and energisation.
Maintain all test equipment, tools, and personal protective equipment in safe and calibrated condition.
Complete all required compliance documentation including Certificates of Compliance, as-built records, and test results.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee high voltage electrician operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Electrical & Low Voltage workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Install, test, commission, and maintain high voltage (HV) electrical systems including 11kV, 33kV, and 110kV switchgear, transformers, and substations.
Carry out HV cable jointing, terminating, and testing for underground and overhead HV distribution and transmission systems.
Install, test, and commission HV switchgear including ring main units (RMU), circuit breakers, disconnectors, and protection relays.
Carry out HV transformer installation, oil sampling, Buchholz relay testing, and tap changer maintenance.
Operate isolating and earthing equipment under live line working procedures and approved safety rules for HV systems.
Install and commission substation protection, control, and SCADA systems for HV network infrastructure.
Carry out HV cable fault location using time domain reflectometry (TDR) and fault burning equipment.
Carry out insulation resistance testing, partial discharge testing, and high voltage withstand testing of HV equipment.
Prepare and implement switching programs, safety isolations, and earthing schemes per utility safety rules.
Read and interpret HV engineering drawings, protection relay settings, and substation layout drawings accurately.
Ensure all HV works comply with Electricity (Safety) Regulations 2010, line owner safety rules, and AS/NZS 3000.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and electricity safety regulations for live line working.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee instrumentation technician operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Electrical & Low Voltage workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.

duties: Install, calibrate, test, commission, and maintain process instrumentation including pressure transmitters, flow meters, temperature sensors, and analysers.
Install field instruments and associated wiring, pneumatic tubing, impulse lines, and instrument supports in process plant environments.
Carry out loop checking, calibration verification, and functional testing of instrumentation and control systems.
Configure and program smart field instruments, PLCs, DCS systems, and SCADA systems using approved configuration tools.
Install and terminate instrument cables in marshalling cabinets, JBs, and control room panels per wiring schedules.
Carry out fault diagnosis and repair of failed instrumentation using calibration equipment, multimeters, and HART communicators.
Install and commission safety instrumented systems (SIS) and safety integrity level (SIL) rated instrumentation.
Carry out functional safety testing of SIS loops per IEC 61511 and the SIL verification documentation.
Read and interpret P&IDs, loop diagrams, wiring schedules, and instrument data sheets accurately.
Complete instrument index updates, calibration records, and as-built documentation for all installed instruments.
Ensure all instrumentation works comply with applicable hazardous area classification (ATEX/IECEx) requirements.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, permit to work systems, and process safety requirements.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee solar / renewable energy installer operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.

duties: Install, test, and commission grid-connected and off-grid solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on residential, commercial, and industrial buildings.
Install solar PV panels, mounting systems, racking, and roof penetrations per manufacturer specifications and AS/NZS 5033.
Install inverters, battery energy storage systems (BESS), switchboards, and metering equipment for solar PV installations.
Install and terminate DC and AC wiring for solar PV systems per AS/NZS 3000 and AS/NZS 5033.
Carry out installation of EV charging stations and associated switchboard modifications.
Carry out solar resource assessment, shading analysis, and system sizing for proposed solar PV installations.
Commission and test solar PV systems including insulation resistance testing, IV curve tracing, and performance verification.
Liaise with lines companies for G59 protection relay settings, export limiting, and grid connection approvals.
Register solar PV installations with the Electricity Authority and lines company per NZ connection requirements.
Complete all required Certificates of Compliance (CoC) and system commissioning documentation.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and working at heights regulations.
Maintain all installation tools, test equipment, and personal protective equipment in a safe and calibrated condition.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee boilermaker operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Fabricate, assemble, repair, and maintain pressure vessels, boilers, tanks, heat exchangers, and process vessels to ASME and NZ pressure vessel standards.
Read and interpret engineering drawings, vessel data sheets, and fabrication specifications to plan and execute boilermaking works.
Lay out, cut, form, and fit steel plate and sections for pressure vessel fabrication using thermal cutting, press brakes, and rolling machines.
Carry out pressure vessel welding using approved welding procedures (WPS) and qualified welders per AS/NZS 3992 or ASME IX.
Conduct NDT including radiography, ultrasonic testing, magnetic particle inspection, and dye penetrant testing on pressure vessel welds.
Carry out hydrostatic pressure testing of pressure vessels, piping, and heat exchangers to specified test pressures.
Install pressure vessel fittings, nozzles, manholes, flanges, and process connections per vessel design.
Carry out structural steel fabrication and installation of vessel support structures, saddles, skirts, and legs.
Repair and recondition pressure vessels, tanks, and boilers including plate replacement, weld repair, and lining installation.
Ensure all pressure vessel fabrication and repair complies with NZ Pressure Equipment, Cranes and Passenger Ropeways Regulations 1999.
Maintain all fabrication tools, welding equipment, and NDT equipment in a safe and calibrated condition.
Comply with all site and workshop Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space entry procedures.

Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Plan, rig, and execute lifts of heavy plant, equipment, structural steel, and prefabricated modules using cranes, hoists, and rigging equipment.
Select appropriate lifting equipment including slings, shackles, hooks, spreader beams, and lifting frames for each specific lift.
Calculate load weights, lift angles, and rigging configurations to ensure safe lift execution within equipment SWL ratings.
Prepare and submit lift plans, method statements, and risk assessments for complex and critical lifts.
Direct crane operators, signal lift movements, and coordinate personnel during lifting operations.
Install, dismantle, and maintain rigging equipment, chain blocks, lever hoists, and tirfors for skidding and jacking operations.
Carry out skidding, jacking, rolling, and shifting of heavy equipment using hydraulic jacks, skid tracks, and roller systems.
Carry out inspection of all lifting gear, slings, shackles, and accessories and quarantine defective equipment.
Ensure all lifting operations comply with the Health and Safety at Work (Cranes) Regulations and WorkSafe NZ guidelines.
Hold current Rigging Certificate of Competence issued by a recognised NZ authority for the classes of rigging undertaken.
Liaise with crane contractors, structural engineers, and site supervisors to plan and execute lifting operations safely.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Fabricate, form, and install sheet metal products including ductwork, flashings, gutters, downpipes, hoods, enclosures, and architectural metalwork.
Operate sheet metal fabrication machinery including guillotines, press brakes, rollers, plasma cutters, and laser cutting machines.
Lay out, mark, and cut sheet metal components from flat sheet material using templates, drawings, and CAD data.
Form and bend sheet metal components using folder brakes, english wheels, and hand forming tools to specified profiles.
Join sheet metal components using MIG welding, TIG welding, spot welding, riveting, and mechanical fastening methods.

Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Perform structural and general welding operations including MIG, TIG, MMA (stick), and flux-cored arc welding on mild steel, stainless steel, and aluminium.
Fabricate, assemble, and weld structural steel members, frames, beams, columns, brackets, and connection plates per engineering drawings and approved welding procedures.
Set up and operate welding equipment, selecting appropriate welding parameters, polarity, and filler materials for base metal compatibility.
Carry out pre-weld preparation including cutting, grinding, bevelling, and fit-up of components to required joint configurations.
Perform visual inspection and basic non-destructive testing (NDT) of completed welds including visual and dye penetrant inspection.
Ensure all welding works comply with NZS 1554 (Structural Steel Welding), AS/NZS 1665 (Aluminium Welding), and project quality plans.
Read and interpret structural steel drawings, isometric drawings, welding symbols, and fabrication details accurately.
Carry out on-site welding, cutting, and grinding works including oxy-fuel cutting and plasma cutting operations safely.
Monitor and control weld quality including preheat, interpass temperature, heat input, and post-weld treatment.
Maintain welding equipment, gas cylinders, personal protective equipment, and tools in safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements including hot work permit procedures and fire watch obligations.
Complete all required welding inspection records, weld maps, and quality documentation per the project quality plan.

duties: Install suspended ceiling systems including metal grid systems, acoustic tile ceilings, plasterboard ceilings, and specialty ceiling systems.
Install perimeter angles, main runners, cross tees, and ceiling tile components per manufacturer installation guidelines.
Install acoustic ceiling tiles, plasterboard, and specialty ceiling panels to achieve specified fire ratings and acoustic performance.
Fix plasterboard to timber and steel stud framing for ceilings, bulkheads, and soffits.
Install shadow line, cove, and feature ceiling systems for architectural and decorative applications.
Carry out stopping, taping, and finishing of plasterboard ceiling joints to Level 4 or Level 5 finish standards.
Install ceiling access panels, recessed lighting frames, HVAC diffuser frames, and other integrated ceiling elements.
Co-ordinate ceiling installation with mechanical, electrical, fire protection, and AV services above the ceiling void.
Read and interpret reflected ceiling plans, services coordination drawings, and specifications accurately.
Ensure all ceiling works comply with fire resistance rating requirements under NZBC C3 and acoustic performance specifications.
Maintain all ceiling fixing tools, power tools, and equipment in a safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Install, lay, and finish resilient floor coverings including vinyl sheet, LVT, LVP, rubber, and linoleum flooring to manufacturer specifications.
Install, sand, and finish timber and engineered timber flooring systems using secret nailing, glue-down, and floating installation methods.
Lay ceramic, porcelain, and natural stone tiles on floors, walls, and wet area surfaces.
Prepare substrates for flooring installation including grinding, levelling, patching, and applying self-levelling compounds.
Install carpet and carpet tiles including stretching, seaming, cutting, and fixing using power stretchers and adhesives.
Install transition strips, Scotia beading, stair nosings, and other accessory items to complete flooring installations.
Read and interpret flooring layout drawings, specifications, and material schedules accurately.
Carry out moisture testing and assessment of substrates prior to installation.
Ensure all flooring works comply with manufacturer installation requirements and NZ standards.
Select appropriate adhesives, fasteners, and installation methods for substrate type and flooring material.
Maintain all tools, equipment, and materials in a clean, safe, and organised condition on site.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Prepare interior and exterior surfaces for painting including filling, sanding, washing, priming, and applying barrier coatings.
Apply paint, stain, varnish, lacquer, and other protective and decorative coatings using brushes, rollers, and spray equipment.
Carry out wallpapering, texture coating, and decorative finishes including Venetian plaster and feature wall techniques.
Erect and safely use scaffolding, trestles, extension ladders, and elevated work platforms to access elevated areas.
Identify and remediate defects including efflorescence, rust, mould, and moisture damage prior to coating application.

Apply protective coatings to structural steel, pipes, tanks, and industrial equipment per corrosion protection specifications.

duties: Apply solid plaster, wet plaster, and texture coatings to interior and exterior wall and ceiling surfaces per NZBC and project specifications.
Install, fix, and finish plasterboard (GIB) systems including stopping, taping, sanding, and finishing to Level 4 or Level 5 standards.
Apply exterior plaster cladding systems including Rockcote and similar proprietary systems per manufacturer requirements.
Carry out fibrous plaster installation including cornices, columns, ceiling roses, and decorative mouldings.
Prepare and apply render systems to masonry, concrete, and fibre cement substrates including scratch, brown, and finish coats.
Install expansion joints, control joints, and beads in plaster systems to manage movement and prevent cracking.

Identify and remediate substrate defects, moisture damage, and cracking prior to plaster application.
Ensure all plastering works comply with NZBC, NZS 4251 (Solid Plastering), and site-specific weathertightness requirements.
Operate and maintain all plastering tools, spray equipment, and mixing equipment in a safe and clean condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE.
Complete all required inspection records and participate in quality hold point inspections at key stages.

Apply waterproof render and tanking systems to basement walls, retaining walls, and below-ground structures.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, render specifications, and product data sheets accurately.
Ensure all rendering works comply with NZBC, NZS 4251 (Solid Plastering), and manufacturer specifications.
Operate rendering machines, spray pumps, and mixing equipment safely per manufacturer instructions.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee wall & floor tiler operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Finishing Trades workstream.

Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Install ceramic, porcelain, natural stone, glass, and mosaic tiles on floors, walls, and wet areas in residential and commercial projects.
Prepare substrates for tiling including grinding, screeding, waterproofing, and priming to achieve flat and level surfaces.
Set out tile layouts, establish datum lines, and plan tile patterns to minimise cuts and optimise appearance.
Mix and apply adhesive mortars, epoxy adhesives, and tile adhesives appropriate to substrate type and tile size.
Cut tiles using wet saws, angle grinders, and tile cutters for accurate fits around penetrations and obstacles.
Apply grout, silicone sealants, and movement joint filler to all tiled surfaces to specified joint widths.
Install wet area waterproofing membranes under tile systems in bathrooms, showers, and laundries per AS 3740.
Install heated floor systems and membranes under tile systems in conjunction with tiling works.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, tile layout drawings, and specifications accurately.
Ensure all tiling works comply with AS 3958 (Ceramic Tiles), wet area waterproofing requirements, and project specifications.
Maintain all tiling tools, mixers, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Install thermal and acoustic insulation systems in walls, floors, ceilings, and roof spaces per NZBC H1 and project specifications.
Install bulk insulation products including glass wool batts, polyester batts, and rigid foam boards in timber and steel framed construction.
Install blown loose-fill insulation in ceiling spaces using specialist blowing equipment to specified depths and R-values.
Install reflective foil insulation and vapour control layers in walls, roofs, and subfloor spaces.
Install acoustic insulation batts in internal wall, floor, and ceiling systems to achieve specified sound transmission ratings.
Install underfloor insulation systems including polystyrene boards, glass wool batts, and reflective foil.
Carry out retrofit insulation installations in existing buildings including access through ceiling manholes and subfloor spaces.
Ensure all insulation works comply with NZBC H1 Schedule 1, NZS 4218 (Thermal Insulation), and project energy modelling specifications.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, energy reports, and specifications to determine correct insulation products and R-values.
Install pipe and duct insulation on HVAC, plumbing, and mechanical services pipework.
Maintain all insulation installation equipment, blowing machines, and tools in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including respiratory protection.

duties: Install passive fire protection systems including fire stopping, intumescent seals, fire collars, fire pillows, and mineral wool infill around service penetrations through fire-rated elements.
Apply intumescent coatings and fire-resistant mastics to structural steel members to achieve specified fire resistance ratings (FRR) per NZBC C3.
Install fire-rated board systems and encasement systems around structural steel, columns, and beams.
Install fire-rated access panels, fire doors, fire dampers, and associated seals and hardware in fire-rated construction.
Carry out linear joint sealing in movement joints and perimeter gaps between fire-rated elements.
Apply fire-rated spray compounds and ablative coatings to structural steel and concrete elements.
Carry out fire-rated penetration sealing for all services including pipes, cables, cable trays, ductwork, and conduits.
Inspect installed passive fire protection works and complete inspection documentation for building consent authority verification.
Read and interpret fire engineering reports, passive fire protection schedules, and construction drawings accurately.
Ensure all passive fire protection works comply with NZBC C clauses, fire engineer specifications, and manufacturer system certification.
Maintain all application tools, equipment, and materials in a safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee irrigation technician operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Landscaping & External Works workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Design, install, commission, and maintain irrigation systems for residential gardens, sports fields, commercial landscapes, and agricultural applications.
Install drip irrigation, micro-spray, and pop-up sprinkler systems with appropriate spacing, heads, and coverage patterns.
Install and commission solenoid valves, master valves, flow sensors, and weather-based irrigation controllers.
Install underground irrigation mainlines, lateral lines, and fittings using HDPE, PVC, and poly pipe.
Commission and programme irrigation controllers, smart controllers, and remote monitoring systems.
Carry out hydraulic calculations for irrigation system design including pipe sizing, pressure loss, and sprinkler selection.
Carry out installation of potable water backflow prevention devices per NZ drinking water standards.
Diagnose and repair irrigation system faults including broken heads, valve failures, controller faults, and pipe leaks.
Carry out system auditing and water use efficiency assessments on existing irrigation systems.
Carry out seasonal winterisation and start-up of irrigation systems including blow-out and pressure testing.
Read and interpret landscape drawings, irrigation design drawings, and site plans accurately.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Install, commission, test, and maintain fire sprinkler systems, fire hydrant systems, hose reel systems, and deluge systems per NZS 4541 and NZS 4510.

Cut, bevel, fit, and join process pipes using welding, flanging, threading, and mechanical coupling methods per pressure rating.

Carry out hydrostatic pressure testing, pneumatic testing, and leak testing of completed pipework to specified test pressures.
Install valves, instrumentation, flow meters, safety relief valves, and process equipment within piping systems.
Apply thermal insulation, cladding, and heat tracing to process pipework for temperature control and personnel protection.

Ensure all process pipework complies with ASME B31.3 (Process Piping) or applicable NZ standards and P&ID requirements.
Participate in pre-commissioning, commissioning, and start-up activities for new process facilities.
Maintain all pipefitting tools, measuring equipment, and personal protective equipment in a safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, permit to work systems, and process safety requirements.

duties: Install kitchen cabinetry, bathroom vanities, laundry units, and custom joinery in residential, commercial, and hospitality fit-out projects.
Install wardrobe systems, built-in shelving, study furniture, and custom storage solutions to builder drawings and client specifications.
Install benchtops including laminate, solid surface, stone, and timber benchtops with accurate scribe fits and cutouts.
Carry out installation of appliances including ovens, cooktops, dishwashers, and range hoods in cabinetry.
Install glass splashbacks, mirrored panels, and decorative glass in kitchen and bathroom fit-outs.
Adjust cabinet doors, drawer runners, hinges, and hardware to achieve smooth operation and flush alignment.
Carry out on-site modifications to cabinetry including scribing to walls, cutting out for plumbing, and fitting cornices.
Install shop joinery, reception counters, display units, and commercial fit-out joinery in retail and hospitality projects.
Read and interpret joinery shop drawings, kitchen plans, and elevation drawings accurately.
Ensure all joinery installation is square, plumb, and level, and complies with relevant NZ Standards.
Maintain all installation tools, power tools, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Install, commission, test, and maintain passenger lifts, goods lifts, escalators, moving walkways, and vertical platform lifts.
Install lift guide rails, car slings, counterweights, traction machines, hydraulic cylinders, and associated equipment in lift shafts.
Install lift control panels, door operators, car operating panels, hall call buttons, and safety circuits.
Commission and test lift installations including safety device testing, load testing, and performance verification.
Carry out routine maintenance, safety inspections, and statutory testing of lifts and escalators per AS 1735 requirements.
Diagnose and repair lift and escalator faults including motor, controller, safety device, and door system failures.
Carry out modernisation works on existing lifts including controller upgrades, door system replacement, and machine upgrades.
Ensure all lift works comply with AS 1735 (Lifts, Escalators and Moving Walks), NZBC, and Worksafe NZ requirements.
Liaise with building owners, facility managers, and building consent authorities for lift inspections and annual safety certificates.
Read and interpret lift installation drawings, wiring diagrams, and maintenance manuals accurately.
Maintain all lift installation tools, test equipment, and PPE in a safe and calibrated condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space procedures for working in lift pits.

duties: Carry out installation of precast concrete elements including wall panels, columns, beams, stairs, landings, and hollow-core floor units.
Rig and lift precast concrete elements using cranes, lifting inserts, and specialist spreader beams per precast erection drawings.
Align, plumb, and level precast elements using grout pads, temporary props, and alignment tools to specified tolerances.
Install and tension temporary propping and bracing systems to support precast walls and columns prior to structural connections.
Grout column base plates, connection pockets, and joints between precast elements per structural engineer specifications.
Carry out structural connections between precast elements including welding of embedded plates, bolting, and grouted connections.
Install seismic connections, ductile connections, and movement joints in precast structures per the seismic design.
Carry out tilt-up concrete panel casting, lifting, and erection on commercial projects.
Operate cranes, elevated work platforms, and man cages safely when working at height on precast erection.
Read and interpret precast erection drawings, connection details, and lift sequences accurately.
Ensure all precast installation works comply with structural engineer specifications, NZS 3101, and seismic design requirements.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Install exterior wall cladding systems including fibre cement, weatherboard, aluminium composite panels (ACP), and curtain wall systems.
Install cavity batten systems, rigid air barriers, and building wraps per NZBC E2 and manufacturer requirements.
Install Rockcote, Sto, and similar proprietary exterior insulation and finish systems (EIFS) per CodeMark conditions.
Cut, fix, and finish fibre cement sheet, Linea weatherboard, and similar products to specified profiles and fixing patterns.
Install aluminium composite panel (ACP) systems including subframe, cassette panels, and wet-sealed joint systems.
Install rainscreen cladding systems including terracotta, fibre cement, and metal rainscreen panels on commercial facades.
Flash and seal all cladding junctions, penetrations, windows, doors, and abutments to achieve weathertight performance.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, facade specifications, and cladding system installation manuals accurately.
Ensure all cladding works comply with NZBC E2 (External Moisture) and relevant CodeMark or Technical Appraisal conditions.
Carry out weathertightness testing including water testing, hose testing, and chamber testing of installed cladding systems.
Operate scaffolding, mast climbers, and elevated work platforms safely when working at height on facades.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

duties: Cut, shape, handle, and install glass panels, double-glazed units (IGUs), laminated glass, toughened glass, and specialty glass products.

Install and seal glazing units using appropriate setting blocks, glazing tapes, structural silicone, and weatherseal systems.
Carry out glass replacement, resealing, and remediation works on existing windows, doors, and glazed facades.
Install glass balustrades, frameless shower screens, glass splashbacks, and decorative glass features.
Carry out site measurement, template making, and survey for custom glazing fabrication orders.
Handle and transport large glass panels using suction lifters, A-frames, and mechanical handling equipment safely.

Read and interpret architectural drawings, glazing schedules, and curtain wall drawings accurately.
Install framing, mullions, transoms, and support systems for glazing as required by the curtain wall or window system design.
Maintain all glazing tools, cutting equipment, and handling equipment in a safe and operational condition.

duties: Apply waterproofing and tanking systems to wet areas, basements, podium decks, balconies, roofs, and below-ground structures per NZBC E2, E3, and manufacturer specifications.
Install liquid-applied waterproofing membranes including polyurethane, acrylic, bituminous, and cementitious systems.
Install sheet membrane waterproofing systems including torch-on bituminous membranes, TPO, EPDM, and PVC membrane systems.
Prepare substrates for waterproofing application including grinding, patching, priming, and applying crack bridging treatments.
Install wet area waterproofing systems in bathrooms, showers, laundries, and commercial kitchens per AS 3740 and NZBC.
Install drainage cells, protection boards, and filter fabric over waterproofing membranes in below-ground applications.
Carry out flood testing, water ponding tests, and adhesion testing on completed waterproofing systems.
Apply joint sealants, expansion joint covers, and penetration seals as part of the complete waterproofing system.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, waterproofing specifications, and manufacturer data sheets accurately.
Ensure all waterproofing works comply with NZBC, relevant NZ Standards, and manufacturer CodeMark or Appraisal conditions.
Maintain all spray equipment, application tools, and safety equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including respiratory protection.

duties: Install, repair, and maintain metal roofing systems including corrugated iron, standing seam, concealed fix, and long-run roofing profiles to NZBC E2 requirements.

Operate and maintain all roofing tools, bending machines, and power tools in a safe and operational condition.
Complete all required compliance documentation and participate in weathertightness inspections at key hold points.

duties: Lay, bond, and finish brick, block, stone, and masonry units to construct walls, piers, columns, and arches for structural and non-structural applications.
Mix, apply, and finish mortar and grout using appropriate mix designs for substrate type, environment, and structural requirements.
Install and align reinforcing steel, ties, anchors, and connectors within masonry systems per structural engineer specifications.
Construct cavity wall systems, veneer cladding, and fire-rated masonry walls including wall ties, DPC, and weep holes.
Carry out restoration and remediation works on heritage masonry buildings including tuckpointing, repointing, and unit replacement.
Install lintels, sills, copings, corbels, and other masonry elements within brickwork and blockwork construction.
Lay paving, paths, driveways, and hardscaping using brick, clay pavers, concrete pavers, and natural stone.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, masonry layout plans, and specifications accurately.
Ensure all masonry works comply with NZS 4210 (Masonry Construction), NZBC, and structural engineer specifications.
Erect and use scaffolding, trestles, and working platforms safely per HSWA 2015 working at heights requirements.
Maintain all masonry tools, mixers, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements and participate in quality inspections at structural masonry hold points.

duties: Carry out structural timber framing, formwork, and rough carpentry works on residential and commercial construction sites in accordance with project plans and NZBC requirements.
Install, fit, and fix wooden structures including wall frames, roof trusses, flooring systems, and subfloor frameworks as per engineering specifications.
Install interior and exterior joinery including doors, windows, skirting boards, architraves, and built-in cabinetry to specified tolerances.
Read, interpret, and work from architectural drawings, construction plans, and technical specifications to ensure accurate execution of carpentry tasks.
Measure, mark, cut, and shape timber and timber-based materials using hand tools and power tools including saws, routers, nail guns, and pneumatic equipment.
Ensure all carpentry works comply with the New Zealand Building Code (NZBC), relevant New Zealand Standards (NZS), and site-specific safety requirements.
Erect and dismantle temporary structures, formwork, scaffolding supports, and hoarding as required during construction phases.
Carry out weatherboard cladding, fascia, soffit, and exterior finishing works to manufacturer specifications and weathertightness standards.
Inspect completed works for quality assurance, identify defects, and carry out remedial or rectification works as directed.
Complete all required site documentation including timesheets, work orders, and compliance checklists, and report progress to the Site Supervisor.
Maintain all hand tools, power tools, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition and report faults promptly.
Adhere to all company Health and Safety policies, HSWA 2015 obligations, and site safety plans including use of appropriate PPE at all times.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee concreter operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Erect, align, and strip formwork for concrete slabs, footings, foundations, columns, beams, retaining walls, and other structural elements.
Place, compact, level, and finish concrete using hand screeds, power screeds, floats, trowels, and vibrators to achieve specified surface finishes.
Install reinforcing steel (rebar) and mesh, including cutting, bending, tying, and placing rebar cages per structural engineer specifications.
Carry out concrete cutting, coring, breaking, and demolition works using diamond saws, core drills, and jackhammers.
Apply surface treatments and finishes to concrete including broomed, exposed aggregate, polished, and spray-on textured finishes.
Install expansion joints, saw-cut joints, crack inducers, and waterstops in concrete slabs and structures.
Operate concrete pumps, agitator trucks, kibbles, and placing booms to accurately place concrete in formwork.
Read and interpret structural drawings, formwork drawings, and concrete specifications accurately.
Ensure all concrete works comply with NZS 3109 (Concrete Construction), NZS 3101, and project quality requirements.
Carry out concrete curing procedures including wet curing, curing compounds, and insulation blankets.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Complete all required site documentation and quality records for concrete placement works.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee formwork carpenter operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Design, fabricate, erect, and strip all types of concrete formwork systems including engineered panel systems, traditional timber formwork, and proprietary shutter systems.
Erect falsework, propping, and shoring systems for elevated slabs, suspended floors, bridges, and transfer structures to engineer-approved designs.
Read and interpret formwork design drawings, shoring plans, and structural engineer specifications to plan and execute formwork works safely.
Install waterstops, cast-in items, starter bars, sleeves, and penetrations within formwork prior to concrete placement.
Strip and clean formwork and falsework components after concrete has achieved specified stripping strength.
Carry out formwork inspections prior to concrete placement and participate in engineer hold point checks.
Ensure all formwork and falsework complies with AS/NZS 3610 (Formwork for Concrete) and the structural engineer design.
Operate cranes, forklifts, and elevated work platforms to handle and position formwork components safely.
Maintain formwork panels, props, and components in a clean and serviceable condition and report damaged components.
Carry out layout, survey, and control of formwork positions and levels using levels and theodolites.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space procedures.
Complete all required formwork inspection records and documentation as required by the project quality plan.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee steel fixer / reinforcing iron worker operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Cut, bend, shape, and place reinforcing steel bars (rebar) and mesh in accordance with structural drawings, bar bending schedules, and engineer specifications.
Assemble and tie rebar cages, mats, and frames for footings, slabs, columns, beams, walls, and bridge decks using approved tie wire and bar chairs.
Install and fix post-tensioning and pre-tensioning systems including duct placement, anchorage installation, and sheathing as per post-tensioning drawings.
Operate rebar bending machines, cutting machines, and mechanical splicing equipment safely and in accordance with manufacturer guidelines.
Position and secure reinforcement to specified cover dimensions using bar chairs, spacers, and supports to ensure correct concrete cover.
Read and interpret structural drawings, reinforcement layout plans, and bar bending schedules to plan and execute reinforcing works accurately.
Carry out inspection of placed reinforcement and prepare for engineer hold point inspections prior to concrete placement.
Ensure all reinforcing works comply with NZS 3101 (Concrete Structures), project quality plans, and structural engineer requirements.
Erect and dismantle formwork, falsework, and support systems as required in conjunction with reinforcing placement.
Operate elevated work platforms, cranes, and lifting equipment in a safe and certified manner as required on site.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Complete all required site documentation, inspection records, and report deficiencies or non-conformances to the Site Supervisor promptly.

duties: Cut, dress, shape, and install natural stone including granite, marble, limestone, sandstone, and bluestone for architectural, heritage, and decorative applications.
Fabricate stone cladding panels, copings, sills, lintels, columns, and decorative features using hand tools and stone cutting machinery.
Install stone cladding systems on building facades including dry-fix and mortar-fix systems with appropriate anchoring details.
Carry out restoration and conservation of heritage stonework including cleaning, repointing, and replacement of damaged stone.
Lay natural stone flooring, paving, and landscaping using appropriate bedding, jointing, and sealing systems.
Carve decorative stonework, inscriptions, memorials, and artistic features using hand and pneumatic tools.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, stone fabrication drawings, and heritage conservation specifications accurately.
Ensure all stonework complies with relevant New Zealand Standards and heritage conservation requirements.
Select appropriate stone materials, adhesives, mortars, and fixing systems for the application and environment.
Operate lifting equipment and vacuum lifters to handle and position large stone panels safely.
Maintain all stone cutting tools, polishing equipment, and machinery in safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including dust extraction.

Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Erect, assemble, and connect structural steel members including columns, beams, trusses, purlins, and bracing systems for commercial, industrial, and infrastructure projects.
Read and interpret structural steel drawings, connection details, and erection sequences to plan and execute steelwork in accordance with engineer specifications.
Operate cranes, hoists, and rigging equipment to lift and position steel members safely, in accordance with NZ crane operator licensing requirements.
Install and tighten high-strength structural bolts, set bearing plates, and grout column base plates to specified torque values and tolerances.
Carry out field welding of structural connections using MIG, MMA, or flux-cored arc welding as required by the structural design.
Ensure all structural steel erection complies with NZS 3404 (Steel Structures Standard) and structural engineer specifications.
Erect and use temporary bracing, guys, and support systems to maintain stability of the structure during erection.
Carry out plumbing, levelling, and alignment of steel members using theodolites, levels, and measuring equipment.
Operate elevated work platforms, man cages, and fall arrest systems safely when working at height.
Inspect erected steelwork for compliance with drawings and specifications, and report non-conformances to the Structural Engineer.
Maintain all erection tools, equipment, and rigging gear in a safe and serviceable condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and working at heights regulations at all times.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee construction project manager operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Supervision & Management workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Lead and manage all aspects of construction project delivery including scope, programme, cost, quality, safety, and stakeholder management.
Develop, maintain, and report on the construction programme using Gantt charts, CPM schedules, and project management software.
Prepare and manage the project budget, track costs against budget, and prepare regular cost reports for client and management.
Manage design consultants, engineers, surveyors, and specialist subconsultants to deliver design information on programme.
Prepare and issue subcontract packages, assess tender returns, and award subcontracts per company procurement procedures.
Manage subcontractor performance including programme compliance, quality of works, safety performance, and commercial management.
Lead project meetings including design progress meetings, site meetings, health and safety meetings, and client update meetings.
Identify, assess, and manage project risks, opportunities, and issues throughout the project lifecycle.
Manage the building consent process including consent applications, inspections, and code compliance certificates (CCC).
Ensure all construction works comply with the Building Act 2004, NZBC, HSWA 2015, and all relevant legislation and specifications.
Prepare and negotiate project variations, extensions of time claims, and final accounts with clients and subcontractors.
Manage project close-out including practical completion, defect rectification, as-built documentation, and final certification.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee health & safety officer operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.

duties: Develop, implement, and maintain the Site-Specific Safety Plan (SSSP) and associated health and safety management documentation per HSWA 2015.
Conduct daily site safety inspections and audits to identify hazards, unsafe acts, and non-compliances, and issue corrective action reports.
Facilitate toolbox talks, safety inductions, pre-task briefings, and health and safety training programmes for site personnel.
Investigate workplace incidents, near misses, and accidents and prepare incident investigation reports with corrective actions.
Manage the hazard identification and risk assessment process including completing HIRAC documentation for all high-risk work.
Monitor compliance with critical risk controls for Top 5 WorkSafe NZ construction risks including working at heights and excavation.
Liaise with WorkSafe NZ inspectors, client safety representatives, and principal contractors on health and safety matters.
Manage the health monitoring programme including audiometric testing, dust monitoring, and plant operator health surveillance.
Maintain all site safety registers including hazardous substances register, plant register, incident register, and training records.
Carry out safe work method statement (SWMS) reviews and approvals for high-risk construction work (HRCW).
Monitor and enforce PPE compliance, site access controls, and emergency response readiness on site.
Prepare monthly health and safety performance reports including leading and lagging indicators for project management.

duties: Prepare detailed bills of quantities, cost plans, and cost estimates for construction projects at all stages from concept to construction.
Carry out tender document preparation including bills of quantities, schedules of rates, and procurement documentation.
Evaluate and assess contractor tenders, prepare tender analysis reports, and provide recommendations on contract award.
Administer construction contracts including NZS 3910, NZS 3916, or NEC contracts throughout the construction phase.
Assess and certify contractor progress payment claims and prepare payment recommendations for client certification.
Prepare and negotiate variation orders, assess contractor variation claims, and agree final account settlement.
Prepare project cost reports, cash flow forecasts, and cost-to-complete reports for client and management.
Carry out value engineering studies to identify cost savings without compromising function or quality.
Measure and value works in progress and prepare interim cost and financial reports for project stakeholders.
Assess and advise on contractor extension of time claims, delay costs, and disruption claims.
Prepare life cycle cost analyses and whole-of-life cost assessments for assets and design options.
Provide procurement strategy advice, contract selection, and risk allocation recommendations for construction projects.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee site foreman / supervisor operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Supervision & Management workstream.
