Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Supervise, coordinate, and direct all on-site construction activities including labour, subcontractors, and trades to ensure works are completed on time, within budget, and to required quality.
Plan and sequence daily work activities, allocate tasks to tradespeople and labourers, and monitor productivity against the construction programme.
Conduct daily site safety briefings (toolbox talks), enforce site safety rules, and ensure all personnel comply with HSWA 2015 and the site Safety Plan.
Carry out site inductions for all new workers, visitors, and subcontractors and maintain accurate induction and site access records.
Review and interpret construction drawings, specifications, and engineer instructions to ensure works are executed per design intent and NZBC.
Liaise with the Project Manager, engineers, architects, councils, and building inspectors to coordinate inspections and progress consent hold points.
Monitor and control material deliveries, storage, and usage on site to minimise waste and ensure availability of materials for programmed works.
Identify, document, and manage site risks including non-conformances, defects, variations, and delays and escalate to the Project Manager.
Maintain accurate site records including daily site diaries, weather records, labour records, plant logs, and visitor registers.
Ensure all subcontractors hold current licences, insurance, and Health and Safety documentation prior to commencing works.
Monitor quality of all works against project specifications and hold point inspection requirements, and direct remedial works where required.
Assist in the preparation of practical completion inspections, defect lists, and close-out documentation at project end.

duties: Carry out core Structural Engineer tasks as directed by work orders, specifications, and supervisor instructions.
Apply technical knowledge and practical skills required for the Structural Engineer role.
Inspect own work for quality and compliance with standards before sign-off.
Use tools, equipment, and systems safely and report defects or hazards promptly.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee construction surveyor operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Survey & Engineering Support workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Carry out construction surveying and setting out for buildings, civil infrastructure, roads, and utilities using total stations, GPS/GNSS, and digital levels.
Set out building gridlines, pile positions, column bases, and structural elements to dimensions and tolerances specified in engineering drawings.
Establish and maintain survey control networks including horizontal and vertical control benchmarks throughout the project.
Carry out as-built surveys of completed construction works and prepare as-built drawings and data for record purposes.
Monitor structural movements, settlements, and deformations using survey instruments and automated monitoring systems.
Carry out earthworks volume calculations using DTM software for cut and fill balance and earthwork reconciliation.
Process and manage survey data using CAD software, survey data collectors, and project management systems.
Co-ordinate with design engineers, architects, and construction teams to ensure survey control meets project requirements.
Carry out machine control GPS setup and calibration for earthmoving and paving plant.
Read and interpret civil and structural engineering drawings, survey plans, and legal descriptions accurately.
Ensure all survey works comply with NZ Survey Standards, LINZ requirements, and project tolerances.
Maintain all survey instruments, data collectors, and equipment in a calibrated and operational condition.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee geotechnical driller operations and deliverables in accordance with New Zealand construction and infrastructure standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Survey & Engineering Support workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Operate rotary drilling rigs, percussion drilling rigs, and sonic drilling rigs for geotechnical investigation boreholes, water wells, and monitoring wells.
Carry out soil sampling using SPT, thin wall tube sampling, and bulk sampling methods per geotechnical investigation specifications.
Carry out rock coring using diamond core barrels and retrieve and log rock cores for geotechnical and geological assessment.
Install standpipe piezometers, vibrating wire piezometers, and groundwater monitoring wells in completed boreholes.
Carry out in-situ testing including SPT, pressuremeter tests, vane shear tests, and Lugeon permeability tests in boreholes.
Carry out CPT (Cone Penetration Testing) push tests using truck-mounted or track-mounted CPT rigs.
Backfill and grout exploration boreholes and decommission piezometers per environmental protection requirements.
Maintain drill rods, coring equipment, sampling tools, and drilling rig in a clean, lubricated, and operational condition.
Read and interpret borehole logs, geotechnical investigation plans, and soil and rock descriptions accurately.
Complete accurate borehole and drill hole records, sample logs, and field testing data per project specifications.
Operate drill rigs safely per HSWA 2015 requirements, site traffic management plans, and utility clearance procedures.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee medical laboratory technician operations and deliverables in accordance with healthcare and clinical standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Allied Health workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform medical laboratory technician tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and Health and Disability Services standards and HSWA 2015 requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to allied health work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in medical laboratory technician work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for allied health operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the medical laboratory technician role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee cold store worker operations and deliverables in accordance with logistics and supply chain standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Cold Chain workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform cold store worker tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and HSWA 2015 and transport regulations requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to cold chain work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in cold store worker work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for cold chain operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the cold store worker role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee port operations worker operations and deliverables in accordance with logistics and supply chain standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Shipping & Maritime workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform port operations worker tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and HSWA 2015 and transport regulations requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to shipping & maritime work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in port operations worker work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for shipping & maritime operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the port operations worker role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in port operations worker work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for shipping & maritime operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the port operations worker role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee explosives handler operations and deliverables in accordance with energy and resources standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Mining workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform explosives handler tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and HSWA 2015 and major hazard facility requirements requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to mining work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in explosives handler work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for mining operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the explosives handler role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Oil & Gas workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform petroleum engineer tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and HSWA 2015 and major hazard facility requirements requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to oil & gas work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in petroleum engineer work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for oil & gas operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the petroleum engineer role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for renewable energy operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the wind turbine technician role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee cloud engineer operations and deliverables in accordance with information technology standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Infrastructure & Networks workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform cloud engineer tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and Privacy Act 2020 and organisational security policies requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to infrastructure & networks work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in cloud engineer work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for infrastructure & networks operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the cloud engineer role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee community support worker operations and deliverables in accordance with aged care and disability support standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Community Care workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform community support worker tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and Health and Disability Services Act and HSWA 2015 requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to community care work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in community support worker work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for community care operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the community support worker role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

ANZSCO.note: ABS ANZSCO 2022 — Support coordinators (NDIS context in NZ/AU) grouped under community workers 4117

duties_generic: Perform support coordinator tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and Health and Disability Services Act and local health and safety legislation requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to community care work locally workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in support coordinator work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for community care operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the support coordinator role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under local health and safety legislation obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in dementia care worker work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for residential care operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the dementia care worker role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

Inspect own work for quality and compliance with standards before sign-off.
Use tools, equipment, and systems safely and report defects or hazards promptly.
Communicate progress, issues, and completion status to the supervisor or team lead.
Follow site or workplace policies including PPE, access control, and emergency procedures.
Participate in toolbox talks, briefings, and training as required.
Maintain a clean, organised, and safe work area.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee corporate trainer operations and deliverables in accordance with education and training standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Training & Development workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform corporate trainer tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and Education and Training Act 2020 and HSWA 2015 requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to training & development work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in corporate trainer work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for training & development operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the corporate trainer role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in emergency response officer work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for safety operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the emergency response officer role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.

responsibilities: Manage and oversee retail sales assistant operations and deliverables in accordance with retail and consumer services standards and organisational requirements.
Ensure compliance with all applicable legislation including the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA) and relevant industry regulations.
Collaborate with supervisors, colleagues, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes within the Retail Operations workstream.
Maintain accurate records, documentation, quality checks, and reporting as required by the employer and regulatory bodies.
Supervise, mentor, and support junior staff, apprentices, and new team members on safe and effective work practices.
duties: Perform retail sales assistant tasks as directed, following work instructions, site or workplace policies, and Fair Trading Act and HSWA 2015 requirements.
Read, interpret, and apply procedures, specifications, and checklists relevant to retail operations work in New Zealand workplaces.
Set up, operate, maintain, and shut down tools, equipment, and systems used in retail sales assistant work safely and efficiently.
Inspect completed work for quality, identify defects or hazards, and carry out corrective actions or escalate non-conformances promptly.
Coordinate with team members and supervisors to plan daily tasks, manage materials, and meet productivity and safety targets.
Complete required documentation including timesheets, incident reports, maintenance logs, and compliance records accurately and on time.
Apply infection prevention, food safety, privacy, or other sector controls where required for retail operations operations.
Participate in toolbox talks, inductions, audits, and continuous improvement activities relevant to the retail sales assistant role.
Maintain a clean, organised, and secure work area and store tools and materials in accordance with workplace standards.
Respond to emergencies, faults, or customer needs within scope of training and escalate issues outside competence immediately.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) and safe work methods at all times, reporting hazards under HSWA 2015 obligations.
Support training of new staff and contribute to a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture on site or in the workplace.
